name: Radix Sort
def: A non-comparison sort that orders keys digit by digit using counting/bucketing.
problem: Sort integers/fixed-length keys faster than the O(n log n) comparison lower bound.
mechanism: Stably distribute keys into buckets by one digit at a time (LSD or MSD), passing once per digit; counting sort is the stable per-digit step.
benefits: O(n·k) — linear in n for fixed key width
Beats comparison sorts on large integer sets
limits: Only for keys decomposable into digits
k passes can dominate for wide keys
myths: "Radix beats the n log n bound universally" — only for bounded-width keys, not general comparison
